name: Sourdough Italian Bread
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Plus 1 tsp. grated Romano or Parmesan cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Early in the morning combine the starter, 1 tsp. salt, 1 cup lukewarm water and 1-1/2 cups flour. Beat to blend thoroughly. Cover with a clean towel and set aside for approximately 5 hours.
2. When ready to finish the dough, stir the sponge down. Dissolve the yeast in the lukewarm water and add to the sponge.
3. Now stir in the next 4 ingredients in the order given: first adding 4 cups flour, then using another cup of flour if needed.
4. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured breadboard and knead thoroughly, about 7 to 10 minutes.
5. Return the dough to a buttered bowl, cover with a clean towel, and set aside to rise until doubled, about 1 hour.
6. When doubled, punch the dough down and set aside, covered, for another 1/2 hour.
7. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured board again, and divide into 6 pieces of dough.
8. Roll and stretch each piece of dough into a long rope. Braid 3 of the ropes together for each loaf.
9. Tuck the ends under, and lift each braid into a buttered glass loaf pan. Cover and set aside to rise until doubled again, about 1 hour.
10. Brush loaves with melted butter and sprinkle each loaf with a mixture of 1 tsp. each sesame seed and grated Romano or Parmesan cheese.
11. Bake loaves in a preheated oven at 375°F until browned and done, about 40 to 45 minutes.
